chunks.text: \chapter{Common DOS Commands and their UNIX\/\TM~~Equivalents} %\label{DOS Commands} %following text within table to keep it from breaking across page %boundaries. Appendix \ref{dosequiv} gives common DOS commands and the equivalent commands to perfo

DOS commands and the equivalent commands to perform similar functions using UNIX\/\TM. %\begin{table}[h] \bigskip %\isucaption{Common DOS Commands and their UNIX\/\TM Equivalents} \label{dosequiv} \def\baselinestretch{.7} \begin{center} \begin{tabul

ef\baselinestretch{.7} \begin{center} \begin{tabular}{lll} \hline DOS Command~~~&UNIX\/\TM Command~~~&Function\\ \hline \\ %dvl&qual≡\\ $\backslash$ &/&A UNIX slash is a backslash in DOS. \\ \\ cd&cd&Change directories (move from\\ &&one directory to

Change directories (move from\\ &&one directory to another).\\ \\ copy&cp&Copy a file (or group of files).\\ \\ del&rm&Delete a file (or a group of files).\\ \\ dir&ls&Display the contents of the\\ && current directory.\\ \\ edlin&vi, jove, or emacs&

current directory.\\ \\ edlin&vi, jove, or emacs&Create and edit text.\\ \\ find&grep&List lines in a file that meet\\ &&specified criteria.\\ \\ md&mkdir&Create a new subdirectory under\\ &&the current directory.\\ \\ print&lpr&Queue a file for pri

ent directory.\\ \\ print&lpr&Queue a file for printing.\\ \\ rd&rmdir&Delete (or remove) an existing\\ && directory.\\ \\ ren&mv&Rename a file.\\ \\ sort&sort&Sort lines in a file.\\ \\ type&cat or more&Display the contents of a file.\\ \\ %next lin
ore&Display the contents of a file.\\ \\ %next line forces white space to top of page...can be removed or adjusted \vspace{.8in} \end{tabular} \end{center} %\end{table}
